metadata.author: Micah
metadata.subject: Jehovah’s Reproof of Israel and His Promise of Restoration through the Coming Christ.

5: But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, so little to be among the thousands of Judah, from you there will come forth to Me He who is to be Ruler in Israel; and His goings forth are from of old, from the days of eternity.
And He will stand and shepherd His flock in the strength of Jehovah, in the majesty of the name of Jehovah His God; and they will abide, for now He will be great to the ends of the earth.
6: He has shown you, O man, what is good; and what does Jehovah require of you but to do justice and to love lovingkindness and to walk humbly with your God?
7: Who is a God like You, forgiving iniquity and passing over the transgression of the remnant of His inheritance? He does not retain His anger forever, because He delights in lovingkindness.
He will again have compassion on us; He will tread our iniquities underfoot; and You will cast all their sins into the depths of the sea.

2.text: This points to the divinity and pre-existence of Christ, whose origin is not merely in time at His birth in Bethlehem, but from the eternal past.

8.text: The Lord clarifies that external rituals are secondary to a heart that reflects His character through justice, mercy, and humility.

19.text: A beautiful metaphor for the completeness of God's forgiveness, where sins are removed so thoroughly they can never be retrieved or used against the believer.
